title: Hay-on-Wye, Llanthony Priory & Skenfrith Castle

main_route.excerpt: We left home, S, E, J, C, & breakfasted in a lay by.
10 miles w. of Worcester. We left home, S, E, J, C, & breakfasted in a lay by.
We reached __Hay-on-Wye__ at H.30., 11.45., where we looked at book shops.
The campsite was across the bridge at __Radnor's End__ * * * *
Breakfast outdoors & we drove to __Llanthony Priory__ - Very beautiful.
We are having tea [lunch crossed out] in a lay by. We are near a mill & bridge at __Skenfrith Castle__.

boundary_flags.issue: Page marked boundary_mixed / trip mixed_end: the Skenfrith Castle spread (A_0284) likely also carries the opening of the following trip. Skenfrith is the last stop of THIS trip; no return-home note is recorded before the boundary.

summary: A short two-day weekend trip (1-2 July 2000) by Simon, Eve and the two youngest boys, John and Charles. They drove from Birmingham via a lay-by west of Worcester to Hay-on-Wye for its bookshops, camping overnight at Radnor's End across the river. On the Sunday they visited Llanthony Priory and Skenfrith Castle in the Welsh Marches before heading home.
extraction_notes: The only sketch on this trip (A_0284_L) is a plan-view architectural diagram of Skenfrith Castle (towers, watergate, chapel, kitchens, mill, river), NOT a route-order sketch, so narrative order governs the main route; the sketch corroborates Skenfrith Castle as a visited stop. Travellers narrowed to 'S, E, J, C' (Simon, Eve, John, Charles) per A_0283_L; Thomas, William and Peter are not mentioned. Worcester is recorded only as a distance anchor for the lay-by breakfast ('10 miles w. of Worcester'), treated as an approximate transit_stop. Tretower and Gwent appear only in the castle's historical description ('Rebuilt 1270 in stone (along with Tretower) subduing Gwent') and are NOT visited stops. Return home is inferred (confidence 0.7): no explicit homeward note appears before the trip boundary (mixed_end / A_0284 boundary_mixed). Diary page number 27 is taken from A_0283_R. No sea/tunnel crossings (domestic GB trip).
